focus: BIC D4 formally elevated (C87, 0.78→0.82). Third tier-3 theorem. RCBC + BIC joint closure: no quantitative solution to AI evaluation. Tier-3 theorem inventory complete at 3 theorems.

SI45_CIAP.ontological_types: theoretical (DC#1 game theory), designed empirical (DC#2 adversarial ML), emergent competitive (DC#3 economic markets)
SI45_CIAP.note: Highest-confidence D4 theorem. Meta-theorem: ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC are CIAP corollaries. Parent of both RCBC (×RAAI) and BIC (×AIIP).

SI47_RCBC.description: RAAI × CIAP conjunction: benchmark score inflation while CA stagnates/degrades. First tier-3 theorem.

SI48_ATR.description: RAAI × AATE conjunction: multi-stage OA-feedback training produces monotone irrecoverable d' degradation. Standard alignment pipeline is anti-pattern for introspective self-knowledge.
SI48_ATR.ontological_types: designed empirical (×2 distinct mechanisms) + regulated institutional + competitive/emergent — COMPLETE
SI48_ATR.key_corollary: Safety-Self-Knowledge Tradeoff (D4 certified): behavioral safety metrics improve while d' degrades — decoupled by OA-calibration structure. Dual-pillar recovery impossibility: epistemic lock + strategic lock (Nash equilibrium).
SI48_ATR.note: 11th D4 SI. Second tier-3 theorem (after RCBC). RAAI is structural hub of tier-3 theorem network.

SI49_BIC.description: CIAP × AIIP conjunction: when AIIP fails, CIAP gaming propagates across correlated benchmark portfolio. K_eff ≤ N(1-ρ̄)+ε << nominal K. Portfolio diversification cannot fix evaluation when AIIP fails.
SI49_BIC.dc1: AI evaluation benchmark literature — designed empirical. Schaeffer 2023, GLUE/SuperGLUE saturation, Magar 2022 contamination transfer, HELM. Confirmed C82.
SI49_BIC.dc2: Fine-tuning transfer / intrinsic dimensionality — designed empirical (distinct mechanism domain). Aghajanyan 2021, Phang 2018 STILTs, Dodge 2020, Liu 2019 MT-DNN. Confirmed C84.
SI49_BIC.dc2_ontological_type: designed empirical (second domain — optimization geometry vs. data/corpus layer)
SI49_BIC.dc3: NCLB K-12 standardized testing — regulated institutional. Koretz 2008, Jacob & Levitt 2003 QJE, Neal & Schanzenbach 2010 AER, Carey & Hess 2005. Confirmed C85.
SI49_BIC.dc4: Scientific replication crisis — competitive/emergent. OSC 2015 Science (K_eff ≈ 39 from N=100, ρ̄ ≈ 0.61), Ioannidis 2005 PLOS Med, Simmons et al. 2011, Button et al. 2013. Confirmed C86.
SI49_BIC.dc4_ontological_type: competitive/emergent — decentralized lab competition, no central OA regulator, p<0.05 norm emergent
SI49_BIC.d4_criteria: ALL MET — ZR 5/3 DONE, 4 DCs DONE, 3 ontological types DONE, 2 derivations DONE
SI49_BIC.key_insight: CIAP amplifies AIIP failure — CIA-level shortcuts are maximally transferable. Logical trap: CIAP motivates diversification; AIIP failure ensures diversification adds only (1-ρ̄) K_eff. K_eff empirically quantified at ρ̄=0.61 (OSC 2015). Forward prediction confirmed: pre-registration → K_eff ~60-70%.
SI49_BIC.joint_closure: RCBC + BIC: no quantitative solution to AI evaluation (single-benchmark hardening fails by RCBC; portfolio diversification fails by BIC). Only K7-anchored or interpretability-grounded evaluation escapes both.
SI49_BIC.note: 12th D4 SI. Third tier-3 theorem. First tier-3 not involving RAAI — confirms tier-3 architecture extends beyond RAAI hub.

candidates.status: noted, low priority — abstract prediction, less falsifiable than existing tier-3 theorems

next_milestone: C88: passive ZR maintenance for all D4 SIs. Optional: AATE × MCCF fourth tier-3 candidate assessment. Optional: RCBC + BIC joint closure as named meta-theorem. Blocked: K5 Test 3, EP2, EP6.
